questions.question: What was the name of the ancient civilization?
questions.explanation: This is one of the oldest civilizations in the world.

questions.question: Where were the Harappans located?
questions.correct_answer: The Indus Valley region and beyond
questions.explanation: Their settlements extended from modern-day Pakistan to northwestern India.

questions.question: What was unique about their cities?
questions.correct_answer: Planned cities with efficient water management
questions.explanation: They built large reservoirs, drains, and canals for a steady supply of water.

questions.question: What was the significance of seals found in Harappan settlements?
questions.explanation: Thousands of small seals were used to mark and verify trade transactions.

questions.question: What metal did the Harappans master?
questions.explanation: They developed techniques for working copper, which they later alloyed with tin to create bronze.

questions.question: What was the purpose of the large basin at Lothal?
questions.explanation: This structure received and sent boats for further transportation of goods by sea.

questions.question: How did the Harappans manage their water supply?
questions.correct_answer: Through a combination of natural and artificial reservoirs, canals, and drains
questions.explanation: Their advanced water management system ensured a steady supply of water to their cities.

questions.question: What was the likely cause of the decline of the Harappan civilization?
questions.explanation: A drier phase, reduced rainfall, and the drying up of the Sarasvatī River may have contributed to their downfall.

questions.question: How did the Harappans contribute to Indian civilization?
questions.correct_answer: Their culture and technology survived and were passed on to the next phase of Indian civilization
questions.explanation: The legacy of the Indus Valley Civilization continued to influence Indian society for centuries.

questions.question: What was unique about the Harappan diet?
questions.correct_answer: Diverse and included dairy products, turmeric, ginger, and banana
questions.explanation: Their cooking pots contained a variety of foodstuffs, indicating a sophisticated understanding of cuisine.

important_notes.title: Key concept: Planned cities
important_notes.content: The Harappans built large, well-planned cities with advanced water management systems.

important_notes.title: Importance of seals in trade
important_notes.content: Seals were used to identify goods and traders, facilitating a brisk trade across the region.

important_notes.title: Harappan mastery of copper and bronze
important_notes.content: The Harappans developed techniques for working copper and alloying it with tin to create bronze.

important_notes.title: Water management systems
important_notes.content: Their advanced water management system included reservoirs, canals, and drains for a steady supply of water.

important_notes.title: Climatic change and environmental degradation
important_notes.content: A drier phase, reduced rainfall, and the drying up of the Sarasvatī River may have contributed to their downfall.

important_notes.title: Legacy of the Indus Valley Civilization
important_notes.content: Their culture and technology survived and were passed on to the next phase of Indian civilization.

important_notes.title: Diverse Harappan diet
important_notes.content: Their cooking pots contained a variety of foodstuffs, indicating a sophisticated understanding of cuisine.

important_notes.title: Importance of trade
important_notes.content: The Harappans engaged in brisk trade across the region, exchanging goods and ideas with other civilizations.

important_notes.title: Harappan contributions to Indian civilization
important_notes.content: Their culture, technology, and legacy continue to influence Indian society to this day.

important_notes.title: Key concept: Efficient water management
important_notes.content: The Harappans built large reservoirs, drains, and canals for a steady supply of water in their cities.

facts.fact: The Indus Valley Civilization is one of the oldest civilizations in the world.
Harappan settlements extended from modern-day Pakistan to northwestern India.
Thousands of small seals were found in Harappan settlements, used for trade and identification.
The Harappans developed techniques for working copper and alloying it with tin to create bronze.
Their cities had advanced water management systems, including large reservoirs and drains.
A drier phase, reduced rainfall, and the drying up of the Sarasvatī River may have contributed to their downfall.
The legacy of the Indus Valley Civilization continues to influence Indian society today.
Harappan cooking pots contained a variety of foodstuffs, including dairy products and spices.
Their trade networks extended beyond India, with connections to other civilizations in the region.
The Harappans built large, well-planned cities with advanced water management systems and diverse crafts.
